title: Model-selection policy for ALL CLIs: latest-preferred, family-pure, 4-tier vocabulary, auto-refreshed, conformance-checked — all through the bridge
description:
USER DIRECTIVES (2026-07-02, consolidated): (a) 'use the latest models by querying the model list — newer version usually means better'; (b) 'the same model-selection rule policy must apply to ALL LLM CLIs'; (c) 'it should go through the agent-bridge abstraction layer'; (d) 4-level abstract vocabulary 'top=fable, high=opus, balanced=sonnet, fast=haiku' with DEFAULT TIER = top (user-confirmed); (e) 'agy must not have Claude models — Gemini family only' (family purity); (f) 'automatic check for latest-model support by querying each CLI to keep settings up to date'; (g) 'make sure the CLI interfaces are properly supported'.

LIVE EVIDENCE (2026-07-02): claude 2.1.198 picker OMITS fable though `--model fable` works (query alone misses the newest model); agy classification FLAPPED (identical list → balanced switched Sonnet-4.6→GPT-OSS-120B); codex live query FAILED ('/model picker did not open' → silent detect fallback); agy tier map had Claude/GPT-OSS models (family violation — operator-corrected in manifest via salvaged cycle-447 + catalog on 2026-07-02); DispatchModel only overlays source=='live' so a CLI stuck in detect silently freezes on old models.

DESIGN (TDD red-first; clean code; ONE Strategy pipeline for all CLIs — no per-CLI forks outside the strategy registry; Rule 5: judgment in LLM, determinism in Go):

D1 CANDIDATE UNION: per-CLI candidates = live query list UNION policy.json `catalog.extra_models` (per-CLI tokens, e.g. claude:[fable]); every extra is ACCEPTANCE-PROBED through the bridge (one-shot `--model <tok>` dispatch must succeed) before entering `available`.
D2 NEWEST-WINS: deterministic Go version comparator picks the newest WITHIN a family/lineage (fable-5 > opus-4.8 > opus-4.6; gpt-5.5 > gpt-5.4; gemini-3.5 > gemini-3.1); handles name-embedded versions, -mini suffixes, (Thinking)/(High) effort parentheticals, unversioned ids (fall back to classifier order, never crash). The LLM classifier's job shrinks to FAMILY/TIER FIT only.
D3 STABLE RECLASSIFICATION: hash the queried list per CLI; unchanged hash => keep previous tier_models verbatim (no LLM call, no flapping). Store the hash in the catalog entry.
D4 PER-CLI PARITY: the SAME pipeline steps (query -> union extras -> family-filter -> classify fit -> newest-wins -> bridge-probe verify -> commit) execute for EVERY CLI via a Strategy interface; matrix test asserts all 4 CLIs run all steps; a CLI whose live query fails degrades LOUDLY to its previous verified entry (never silently to stale detect values) — and newest-wins also governs detect-fallback candidate sets.
D5 REPAIR CODEX LIVE QUERY: the /model picker-open recipe fails under the refresh flow (boot-interstitial timing); fix so codex rejoins source=live.
D6 C1 EVERYWHERE: every model-REACHING probe (extras acceptance, verify-before-commit) dispatches through the agent-bridge; ollama's check stays list-presence metadata (documented C1 exception, MR5) — NEVER a multi-GB pull as a probe.
D7 FAMILY CONSTRAINT: policy.json `catalog.allowed_families` per CLI (agy:[gemini], claude:[claude], codex:[gpt], ollama:[local/any]); classifier candidates are family-filtered BEFORE newest-wins. Cross-family coverage belongs to the cli_fallback chain, never a CLI's own tier map. (Manifest+catalog already operator-corrected for agy; this makes it structural.) NOTE: Gemini 3.5 Pro announced at I/O 2026-05-19, GA slipped to July 2026 — when agy's picker lists it, newest-wins must promote agy top/deep automatically; that is the acceptance scenario for D2+D8.
D8 FRESHNESS TRIGGERS: live refresh fires on (1) CLI VERSION DRIFT (preflight cli-version-drift inventory already detects it — new binary = new models; wire it to force refresh regardless of TTL), (2) TTL expiry (catalog.ttl_hours knob), (3) operator manual. A refresh may only COMMIT a catalog that passed D1-D7; then re-enable `catalog.auto_refresh=true` and DELETE the operator's stopgap fable hand-pin (and update memory/docs).
D9 CLI INTERFACE CONFORMANCE: on version drift (same trigger as D8) run a bridge-driven conformance suite per CLI verifying every manifest-declared interface: boot+prompt marker (exists: bridge-boot), model channel accepts the resolved tier model (one-shot dispatch), /model picker opens+parses (D1 path; codex's silent failure becomes a RED), headless one-shot works (PromptDispatcher seam), exhausted_regex matches a sample wall render, version capture. RED => bench the CLI loudly (existing cli-health bench; chain routes around) + WARN naming the broken contract and the manifest key to update. Interface breakage becomes a preflight fact, not a runtime incident. `evolve doctor conformance <cli>` for manual runs.
TIER VOCABULARY: add `top` to modelcatalog.CanonicalTiers (4 levels: top/high/balanced/fast); accept `high` as input alias of internal `deep` via the existing translateV1TierKey alias layer (zero churn across ~90 profiles); per-CLI tier_models gains a top entry ( = newest verified frontier per family: claude=fable, codex=gpt-5.5, agy=Gemini 3.1 Pro (High) until 3.5 Pro lands, ollama=largest local); DEFAULT TIER = top when profile/advisor is silent; model_tier_envelope validation extended so max:top phases reach the frontier; advisor may emit top; the Realizer already passes any resolved tier through (no change).

ACCEPTANCE: with extra_models={claude:[fable]}, a full live refresh lands claude top/high correctly WITHOUT a hand-pin; re-refresh with unchanged lists is a no-op (call-counting fake proves zero classifier calls); agy tiers are Gemini-only by construction (family filter test) and stop flapping; codex rejoins source=live; version-drift event triggers refresh+conformance; a deliberately broken fake manifest turns conformance RED and benches; comparator unit-tested across real id shapes; `go test -race` + `apicover -enforce` clean on touched packages; docs/architecture/model-discovery-and-catalog.md documents the pipeline + per-CLI channel/conformance matrix.
